LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DAN NOLTING AND PINE GROVE GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO DEFENDANT DAN NOLTING, WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN IN THAT IT FAILS TO IDENTIFY TO WHICH DEFENDANT OR DEFENDANTS EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IS DIRECTED. CCP 430.10(F) AND RULE OF COURT 2.112 (4). AS SUCH, DEFENDANT CANNOT REASONABLY RESPOND. IN ADDITION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED, WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE FOLLOWING ADDITIONAL GROUNDS: AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE WHAT PROPERTY WAS TAKEN AND WHAT FORCE OR FEAR WAS USED TO TAKE IT. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE AN ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP WITH A THIRD PARTY. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER OF DEFENDANT PINE GROVE GROUP, INC. IS OFF CALENDAR BECAUSE PINE GROVE GROUP, INC. HAS BEEN DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE FROM THIS ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DAN NOLTING'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DEFAMATION AND SLANDER, TORTIOUS INTERFERENCE WITH BUSINESS ADVANTAGE, AND TRESPASS. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT NOLTING'S ALLEGED DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS TO LAW ENFORCEMENT ARE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED OTHER DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS THAT ARE NOT BARRED. A DEMURRER TESTS THE SUFFICIENCY OF AN ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION, AND NOT PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT HE CAN DO SO IF LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. FOR THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED A COGNIZABLE CAUSE OF ACTION, AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT HE CAN DO SO IF LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS 30 DAYS TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - MARTIN ENG, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. SPIROS FOUSEKIS OF THE NARAYAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 403-0150 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAN NOLTING's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, and sustained, without leave to amend, as to the third and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege causes of action for defamation and slander, tortious interference with business advantage, and trespass. For the first cause of action, although Defendant Nolting's alleged defamatory statements to law enforcement are barred by the litigation privilege, Plaintiff has pled other defamatory statements that are not barred. A demurrer tests the sufficiency of an entire cause of action, and not part of a cause of action. For the third cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress, and Plaintiff has not demonstrated that he can do so if leave to amend is granted. For the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled a cognizable cause of action, and Plaintiff has not demonstrated that he can do so if leave to amend is granted. DEFENDANT HAS 30 DAYS TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Off Calendar Mar-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARTIN ENG, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
SPIROS FOUSEKIS, ESQ., 650.403.0150, FOR DEFENDANT DAN NOLTING.

RULING: Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT DAN NOLTING's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 30 days leave to amend. As to the first cause of action, Mr. Nolting's communications with law enforcement are barred by the litigation privilege (Hagberg v. California Federal Bank FSB (2004) 32 Cal.4th 39, 364), and Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend to re-allege, if he can do so in good faith, the other defamatory statements made by Mr. Nolting that were included in his original Complaint. As to the second cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege an economic relationship with a third party. As to the third cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. As to the fourth cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege actual damages from the trespass. As to the fifth cause of action, Mr. Eng is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a cognizable cause of action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANTS DAN NOLTING and PINE GROVE GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained as to Defendant Dan Nolting, with twenty days leave to amend. The Complaint is uncertain in that it fails to identify to which defendant or defendants each cause of action is directed. CCP 430.10(f) and Rule of Court 2.112 (4). As such, Defendant cannot reasonably respond. In addition, the demurrer is sustained, with twenty days leave to amend on the following additional grounds: As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege what property was taken and what force or fear was used to take it. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege an economic relationship with a third party. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege outrageous conduct and severe emotional distress. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts to support this cause of action. The demurrer of Defendant Pine Grove Group, Inc. is off calendar because Pine Grove Group, Inc. has been dismissed with prejudice from this action.
ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/MJM
